source_rationale: CD69 is a canonical early T cell activation marker expressed on the surface of recently activated lymphocytes
CXCR4 is a chemokine receptor upregulated on activated T cells involved in migration and homing
CCL5 (RANTES) is a chemokine produced by activated T cells that recruits immune cells
KLRB1 (CD161) is a C-type lectin receptor expressed on activated T cells and NK-T cells
Granzyme B is a serine protease in cytotoxic granules of activated T cells mediating target cell apoptosis
Interferon-gamma is a hallmark cytokine produced by activated T cells driving Th1 immune responses
IL7R (CD127) is expressed on activated T cells and is important for T cell survival and homeostasis
IL32 is a pro-inflammatory cytokine preferentially expressed by activated T cells and NK cells
PTPRC (CD45) is a pan-leukocyte marker essential for T cell receptor signaling
CD2 is a cell adhesion molecule on T cells that facilitates T cell activation through LFA-3 binding
TFAP2B is a transcription factor essential for amacrine cell fate specification in the retina
TFAP2A is a key transcription factor required for amacrine and horizontal cell development
GAD1 encodes glutamic acid decarboxylase, marking GABAergic amacrine cells in the retina

PAX6 is a master eye development transcription factor highly expressed in amacrine cells
SCG2 (secretogranin II) is a neuropeptide precursor enriched in amacrine cells reflecting their neurosecretory function

Von Willebrand factor is a canonical endothelial cell marker involved in hemostasis
